accent.note: Hue+chroma repainted per selection from the sprite; theme owns lightness (0.8 dark / 0.56 light).

brand.purpose: Wordmark and splash only — the digital face that stamps the game identity. Never on data or controls.

heading.purpose: h1–h4; panel titles up to the splash headline.

body.purpose: All copy and data; 14px base, line-height 1.5.

label.purpose: The global .label kicker for filter/section group labels; deliberate and sparse.

shadows.name: dropdown
shadows.purpose: Search results and popovers that lift off the surface (light theme: … / 0.2).

shadows.name: panel
shadows.purpose: The side detail panel / mobile bottom-sheet edge (light theme: … / 0.12).

motion.name: transition-fast
motion.purpose: Default control feedback (border/background/color) — keeps the chrome calm.

motion.name: accent-morph
motion.purpose: The signature flourish: the accent ramp sweeps to the selected Digimon's hue rather than cutting.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Expressive ease-out for entrances, stat fills, and the accent morph.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Strongest ease-out for the liveliest moments.

components.name: Segmented Button
components.description: Toolbar/action toggle; the whole toolbar shares one tactile press.

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Pill filter toggle shared by the Tree filter bar and the Field guide; carries aria-pressed.

components.name: Domain Chip
components.description: Display-only badge for a domain hue; label stays --text so color is never the sole channel.

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Search / endpoint field; border warms to accent on focus (plus the global focus ring on keyboard focus).
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="search" placeholder="Filter by name or #…" />

components.name: Accent Checkbox
components.description: Native checkbox with a drawn box; the checked fill follows the live accent (the selected Digimon's color).

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Compact icon control (close / swap / pager); accent-faint wash and press feedback.
components.html: <button class="ds-icon" aria-label="Close"><svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" width="14" height="14"><path d="M4 4 L12 12 M12 4 L4 12" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.6" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg></button>

components.name: Reduced Requirement Chip
components.description: A stat requirement eased by the player's Agent Skills: bond-tinted, reads base -> reduced (base greyed, arrow, reduced bold). The base is never hidden; the block never wraps mid-stat.

components.name: Stepper
components.description: Compact -/value/+ control for a small bounded integer (the 0-4 Agent-Skill stacks in Settings).
components.html: 2

components.name: Info Tip
components.description: A '?' help affordance; a native top-layer popover with an on-demand explanation and an optional foot action (e.g. Open settings). Trigger shown here; the bubble uses surface fill + dropdown shadow.

narrative.overview: Time Stranger Tree is a precise, quiet instrument that borrows all of its color from the Digimon in focus. The housing is a warm graphite stage at near-zero chroma; the single accent is repainted, live, with the selected sprite's own signature hue, so the whole interface morphs as you move through the network. The feeling is a capable field tool with a light sci-fi charge — Digivice / terminal energy, without ever cosplaying it. It is tactile and confident: controls sit on solid graphite fills, the active state is a wash of the live accent, and interactions answer back. Density is high and unafraid because the users are planners who want the data. Motion is reserved for meaning: state, feedback, and the one signature flourish — the chromatic morph.
keyCharacteristics: Borrowed color: one dynamic accent, taken from the selected Digimon's sprite; the graphite chrome never competes.
Dual-theme, single system: a night viewport and a daylight-parchment viewport, same tokens, accent legible in both.
Dense but hierarchical: lots of data, ordered by weight and spacing, never a flat grid.
Tactile & confident: solid fills, accent-soft active states, press feedback, a brisk chromatic morph.
Color is never the only channel: names, markers, and shapes always carry the same meaning.

rules.name: The Borrowed Color Rule
rules.body: The accent is never chosen; it is taken from the Digimon in focus. The chrome stays graphite so the subject can supply the color. Never hard-code a brand hue where --accent belongs.

rules.name: The No Sole-Channel Rule
rules.body: Color never carries meaning alone. Every attribute, resistance, domain accent, and route-step kind is paired with a name, a glyph, or a shape badge. If you remove all color, the interface must still be readable.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy comes from weight and size within Hanken Grotesk, never from adding another body face.

rules.name: The Brand-Face Firewall
rules.body: Chakra Petch is for the wordmark and splash only. It never labels a control, a stat, or a row.

rules.name: The Flat-Stage Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest; if something has a drop shadow it must actually float above the page (a dropdown, the panel).

rules.name: The Top-Layer Popover Rule
rules.body: Any menu or tip that overlays the detail panel or the mobile bottom sheet is a native [popover] in the top layer, never an absolutely-positioned dropdown nested in the top bar — a nested dropdown is capped by its ancestor's stacking context (--z-topbar 20 sits below --z-panel 40) and paints behind the panel on mobile. Position it in JS under its trigger; keep --shadow-dropdown + pop-in.

rules.name: The Both-Numbers Rule
rules.body: A value changed by a player setting shows its origin — base -> reduced, base quiet, result bold — so the arithmetic is visible and the original is never lost.

dos: Do take the accent from the selected Digimon (--accent, repainted by chroma.ts); let the graphite chrome recede so the subject leads.
Do author color in OKLCH and reach for a token before any literal value.
Do pair every color with a second channel — a name, a glyph, or a shape badge.
Do keep components tactile: surface-2 fills, --accent-soft active states, a scale(0.96) press, brisk 130ms transitions.
Do reserve motion for meaning and honor prefers-reduced-motion.
Do build depth by stacking bg → surface → surface-2; add a shadow only when an element genuinely floats.

Do render any menu or tip that overlays the panel or bottom sheet as a native [popover] in the top layer, positioned in JS under its trigger — never an absolutely-positioned dropdown nested in the top bar (it paints behind the panel on mobile).
Do show a player-modified value as base → reduced (base greyed, arrow, result bold); never hide the original, and keep each STAT base → reduced block unbreakable.
Do keep Chakra Petch to the wordmark and splash; everything else is Hanken Grotesk, hierarchy by weight.
donts: Don't ship the generic AI-slop SaaS look: no cream/beige backgrounds, no gradient text, no identical icon-card grids, no tracked-uppercase eyebrow over every section, no hero-metric template.
Don't drift toward a cluttered fan-wiki or a bare spreadsheet dump — the named anti-references.
Don't use a border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't hard-code a brand hue where --accent belongs, or let color be the only thing distinguishing two states.
Don't put a display/second face on data, labels, or controls; and never nest cards inside cards.
